cards.title: Having Children Makes You Smarter
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than non-parents. Years of mental and emotional gymnastics from parenting appear to boost cognitive health.

cards.title: When Your Child Is Spewing Serious Sass
cards.body: Kids start sassing as early as age 3, and big reactions often reinforce the behavior. A counterintuitive response is more effective than dramatic overreactions.

cards.title: Your Anxious Child Isn't the Problem
cards.body: Answering "what if" questions with reassurances actually trains children to ask more anxious questions. Three strategies can help break this unhelpful cycle.

cards.title: I Wish My Son Wanted More Time
cards.body: A parent struggles with an adult son in his late 20s who seems distant. An advice columnist suggests examining whether pressure—even unspoken—might be driving the gap.

cards.title: Family Holiday Anxiety and Real Emergencies
cards.body: A parent learns that worrying about worst-case scenarios doesn't prepare you for real crises. When genuine emergencies happen, you discover inner resilience you didn't know you had.

cards.title: Schools Start Late After Late England Kick-off
cards.body: Some schools are opening late to reduce pressure on parents with children watching a 01:00 World Cup match. Parents have largely supported this child-centered scheduling decision.
